description:
src/lib.rs:49 — pub struct DopplerBackend has no Debug impl. Public types in this workspace pattern (callers commonly hold Arc<dyn SecretStore> but also occasionally hold a concrete DopplerBackend in tests) need Debug.

Issue: any caller who writes `dbg!(&backend)` or `tracing::debug!(?backend, ...)` gets a compile error. They have to either remove the diagnostic or write a wrapper.

Fix: derive Debug, but customize so the token field does not leak. Idiomatic pattern:

    impl std::fmt::Debug for DopplerBackend {
        fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
            f.debug_struct("DopplerBackend")
                .field("project", &self.project)
                .field("config", &self.config)
                .field("name", &self.name)
                .field("token", &"<redacted>")
                .finish()
        }
    }

A blind `#[derive(Debug)]` would print the token contents, which is worse than no Debug at all.
title: DopplerBackend does not derive/impl Debug
